description: User Profile Health Info & Workout Goals — Add height, weight, date of birth, workout goal, and unit preference to user profiles with privacy controls and onboarding prompt

userStories.title: Add WorkoutGoal type, UserProfile health fields, and unit conversion helpers to shared package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the shared types and utility functions so the API and web app can work with health profile data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add WorkoutGoal type to packages/shared/src/types.ts exported as: type WorkoutGoal = 'lose_weight' | 'build_muscle' | 'stay_active' | 'improve_endurance' | 'reduce_stress'

Add optional fields to UserProfile interface: heightCm?: number, weightKg?: number, dateOfBirth?: string, workoutGoal?: WorkoutGoal, unitPreference?: 'metric' | 'imperial', healthProfilePromptDismissed?: boolean

Create packages/shared/src/units.ts with conversion helpers: cmToFeetInches(cm) returning {feet, inches}, feetInchesToCm(feet, inches), kgToLbs(kg), lbsToKg(lbs), and a WORKOUT_GOAL_LABELS map from WorkoutGoal to {label: string, emoji: string}
Export units.ts from packages/shared/src/index.ts
Run 'yarn build' in packages/shared successfully so dist/ is updated
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Must build shared package after changes so downstream workspaces (api, web) can resolve the new types from dist/. The existing index.ts re-exports from types.ts — add a re-export for units.ts too.

userStories.title: Add validation and persistence for health fields in PUT /users/me
userStories.description: As a user, I need the API to accept, validate, and store my health profile data so it persists across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /users/me accepts optional fields: heightCm, weightKg, dateOfBirth, workoutGoal, unitPreference, healthProfilePromptDismissed

Validation: heightCm must be number 50-300, weightKg must be number 10-500, dateOfBirth must be valid ISO 8601 date in the past with user at least 13 years old, workoutGoal must be one of the 5 valid values, unitPreference must be 'metric' or 'imperial', healthProfilePromptDismissed must be boolean
Invalid values return 400 with JSON error body describing which field failed (e.g., {error: 'heightCm must be between 50 and 300'})
Fields with value null are accepted and remove the field from the Firestore document (allow unsetting)
Valid fields are persisted to the Firestore users document and returned in the response
GET /users/me returns all health fields when present
Existing API tests continue to pass (run 'cd services/api && yarn test')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing pattern in services/api/src/routes/users.ts PUT /me handler — add new fields to the updates object with validation. Use getDb() for Firestore access. Import WorkoutGoal type from @burnbuddy/shared for validation.

userStories.title: Ensure health fields are excluded from public profile endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I need my health data to remain private — other users should not see it when viewing my profile.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /users/:uid response does NOT include heightCm, weightKg, dateOfBirth, workoutGoal, unitPreference, or healthProfilePromptDismissed
GET /users/:uid/profile (ProfileStats) response does NOT include any health fields
GET /users/search results do NOT include health fields
GET /users/me still returns all health fields (private to the owner)
Implement field stripping as a reusable helper function (e.g., stripHealthFields) in the users route file
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check all endpoints in services/api/src/routes/users.ts that return user data to non-owners. The GET /:uid, GET /:uid/profile, and search endpoints should strip health fields before sending the response.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for health field validation, persistence, and privacy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need test coverage for the new health profile API behavior to prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file services/api/src/routes/__tests__/users-health.test.ts (or add to existing users test file if one exists)
Tests use the project's existing pattern: vi.hoisted for mock functions, vi.mock for firebase/firestore modules, buildApp() helper for Express app, supertest for HTTP assertions

Test cases for PUT /users/me: valid health fields are saved, invalid heightCm (too low, too high, non-number) returns 400, invalid weightKg returns 400, invalid dateOfBirth (future date, under 13) returns 400, invalid workoutGoal returns 400, invalid unitPreference returns 400, null values clear fields
Test cases for privacy: GET /users/:uid does not include health fields, GET /users/me does include health fields
All tests pass when run with 'cd services/api && yarn test'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact test pattern from existing test files in services/api/src/routes/__tests__/ — use vi.hoisted(() => { ... }) for mock functions, vi.mock('../lib/firebase') and vi.mock('../lib/firestore'), import router after mocks, buildApp() to create Express app, supertest for requests. Call vi.resetAllMocks() in beforeEach.

userStories.title: Add Health & Body section with unit preference to account settings
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and edit my height, weight, date of birth, and unit preference in account settings.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Health & Body' section in apps/web/src/app/(main)/account/page.tsx below the existing profile sections, with a lock icon (🔒) and 'Private — only visible to you' subtitle
Unit preference toggle at top of section: two buttons for 'Metric (kg, cm)' and 'Imperial (lbs, ft/in)', saves immediately via PUT /users/me

Height input: shows cm input when metric, shows feet + inches inputs when imperial. Uses cmToFeetInches/feetInchesToCm from @burnbuddy/shared for conversion. Saves as heightCm via PUT /users/me on blur
Weight input: shows kg input when metric, shows lbs input when imperial. Uses kgToLbs/lbsToKg from @burnbuddy/shared. Saves as weightKg via PUT /users/me on blur
Date of birth: HTML date input (type='date'). Saves as ISO 8601 string via PUT /users/me on change. Shows computed age next to it (e.g., 'Age: 35') when dateOfBirth is set
Default unitPreference auto-detected from navigator.language on first render if not set (en-US, en-GB → imperial, others → metric)
Empty fields show placeholder text like 'Not set'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The account settings page is at apps/web/src/app/(main)/account/page.tsx. It uses React Query mutations (useMutation from @tanstack/react-query) to save via apiPut. Follow the same pattern for new fields. Import conversion helpers from @burnbuddy/shared.

userStories.title: Add Workout Goal selector to account settings
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select my primary workout goal from a visual set of options.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Workout Goal' section in account settings below the Health & Body section
Displays 5 goal options as selectable cards in a responsive grid: 🏃 Lose Weight, 🏋️ Build Muscle, ✨ Stay Active, 💪 Improve Endurance, 🧘 Reduce Stress
Use WORKOUT_GOAL_LABELS from @burnbuddy/shared for label text and emoji
Currently selected goal card has a highlighted border/background (use primary color from theme)
Clicking a goal card saves immediately via PUT /users/me with workoutGoal field
Clicking the already-selected goal deselects it (sends workoutGoal: null to clear)
Shows 'No goal selected' state when no goal is set
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Follow the same mutation pattern as other account settings fields. The goal cards should be visually consistent with the app's Tailwind styling — use the color-primary CSS variable for the selected state.

userStories.title: Add onboarding health profile prompt for new users
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to be prompted to set up my health info and workout goal during onboarding so I get a personalized experience.
acceptanceCriteria: New component apps/web/src/components/HealthProfilePrompt.tsx — a card/modal overlay shown on the dashboard page
Prompt is shown when the user's profile has healthProfilePromptDismissed falsy (undefined or false)
Prompt contains: unit preference toggle, height input, weight input, date of birth input, and the 5 workout goal cards — reusing the same input patterns from account settings
Two buttons at bottom: 'Skip' (sets healthProfilePromptDismissed: true, no other changes) and 'Save & Continue' (saves all filled fields + sets healthProfilePromptDismissed: true)
After skip or save, the prompt disappears and the normal dashboard with Getting Started card is visible

On account settings page, show a subtle banner 'Complete your health profile' linking to the Health & Body section if healthProfilePromptDismissed is true but heightCm, weightKg, dateOfBirth, and workoutGoal are all unset
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The dashboard home page is at apps/web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx. It already conditionally shows GettingStartedCard based on profile.gettingStartedDismissed. Add the HealthProfilePrompt above that with similar conditional logic. The prompt should be a centered card with a clean form layout, not a blocking modal.
